title: calotheca littoralis
intro: Calotheca littoralis, also known as sea lavender, is a flowering plant species that thrives in coastal regions and has been widely used in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Calotheca littoralis has been associated with various health benefits, including the reduction of anxiety and stress, improved sleep quality, and the alleviation of respiratory issues. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
